figures.caption: Fig. 1. Overview of the proposed RAG-based framework for automated code inspection and test case generation. The pipeline begins with dataset ingestion, where (a) the Bug In the Code Stack dataset from [47] is used for automated code inspection, and (b) the benchmark dataset from Wang et al. [11] is used for automated test case generation. External knowledge (e.g., MBPP dataset [48, 49]) is embedded using MiniLM-L6-V2 via the Sentence Transformer library. In the retrieval stage, query vectors are matched with stored embeddings using cosine similarity to identify the top-K relevant documents. These retrieved contexts are then passed to the LLM for code analysis and test case generation, followed by evaluation to measure effectiveness.
Fig. 5. Code Analysis Predictions using GPT-3.5-turbo, where (a) the Bug In the Code Stack dataset from [47] is used as an input dataset
